models_tested.notes: 8 evals × 2 configurations = 16 runs at SKILL.md v0.7, all run_number == 1. Replaces the previous v0.1 Sonnet run set (removed in spec 28 Phase 1). Analyzer is claude-sonnet-4-6 (sole grader for all 32 transcripts) per analyzer-uniformity. Per-run time_seconds, tokens, tool_calls, and errors are extracted from the executor subagent JSONL transcripts (parent-side aggregation of input + output + cache tokens). Two without_skill runs (evals 5 and 8) initially invoked the pr-human-guide skill via the Skill tool and were re-spawned with explicit Skill-tool prohibition; only the clean re-runs are recorded. All eight with_skill runs were also re-spawned: the first batch hit transient 529 errors and was resumed via SendMessage, but the resume path silently inherited the parent's claude-opus-4-7 model rather than the original claude-sonnet-4-6 setting. The recorded runs are the second-pass spawns, verified to have actually executed on claude-sonnet-4-6 by inspecting the JSONL message.model field.

8 evals × 2 configurations = 16 runs at SKILL.md v0.7, all run_number == 1. Analyzer is claude-sonnet-4-6 for analyzer-uniformity. Per-run time_seconds, tokens, tool_calls, and errors are extracted from the executor subagent JSONL transcripts.

notes: Multi-model framing: Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.7 both ran the full 8-eval suite at SKILL.md v0.7. The previous v0.1 Sonnet runs were removed (git history retains the prior shape) so both models share an apples-to-apples skill version.

Per-run time_seconds and tokens are aggregated from the per-agent JSONL transcripts: time = max-minus-min event timestamp; tokens = input + output + cache-creation + cache-read tokens summed across all assistant turns. Cache reads dominate the token count because the prompt is large and re-read each turn.

Sonnet with_skill runs were re-spawned to correct an executor-model mismatch: the first batch hit transient 529 errors at spawn time, was resumed via SendMessage, and the resume path inherited the parent's claude-opus-4-7 instead of the original claude-sonnet-4-6 model. The re-spawns ran cleanly on Sonnet 4.6, verified by inspecting message.model in each agent's JSONL.

Two Sonnet without_skill runs (evals 5 and 8) initially invoked the pr-human-guide skill via the Skill tool despite the SKILL.md/references read prohibition; those runs were re-spawned with explicit Skill-tool prohibition added to the executor prompt, and only the clean re-runs are recorded.

expectations.evidence: '`RENAME COLUMN username TO display_name` — breaks any consumer still selecting `username`'; '`username: String!` → `displayName: String!` is a **breaking change** for every API client'

expectations.evidence: '`ALTER TABLE users DROP COLUMN legacy_id` — **data loss**'; '`legacyId: Int` removed — same concern; any client still requesting it will get a query validation error'

expectations.text: The review guide includes a Concurrency / State section
expectations.evidence: ### Concurrency & shared state header present — accepted variant per grading rules

expectations.text: The review guide flags the module-level shared mutable state (activeJobCount and/or jobQueue) as a concurrency concern
expectations.evidence: activeJobCount and jobQueue are mutated from both the calling thread (via enqueueJob) and from worker `exit` handlers ... Module-level mutable state. `activeJobCount` and `jobQueue` are module-scoped

expectations.text: The review guide flags the new use of worker threads
expectations.evidence: No explicit flag of worker_threads / Worker import as a new/first-use concurrency primitive in the guide body. The discussion of workers is about error handling and lifecycle, not about flagging this as a novel pattern being introduced for the first time.

notes: Without the skill, Opus produced a thorough freeform review with correct concurrency/state content and an accepted section header variant, but missed flagging worker_threads as a new primitive (treated it as a lifecycle/error concern instead) and omitted the required HTML comment markers. Same pattern as sonnet/without.
